user_outcome: Real-binary temporary-Git tests prove complete PASS, failure, tamper, restart, merge, re-plan and ship behavior through public entry points.
rationale: Package tests cannot prove that every adapter reaches the same protocol authorities or that recovery survives process boundaries. This final slice adds no product behavior; it drives the built binary and MCP transport through complete disposable Git histories and fails closed on any divergence from C-01 through C-13 and the v0.16 C-18/C-19 tail.
in_scope: Build the current sworn binary once per conformance test suite and invoke it as a subprocess with isolated HOME, Git config, repositories, worktrees, bare remotes, model-driver fakes, clocks, and environment; no test calls an internal operation as a substitute for its public entry point.

Prove the exact embedded Baton v0.15 pin and isolated Codex/Claude mirror parity, typed-reference ambiguity behavior, live protocol marker gating, canonical semantic identity, report reuse, lifecycle budgets, and S13 cutover through public commands.

Drive sworn loop and maintainability review/adjudicate through exact Baton-plus-overlay PASS/FAIL, exact empty-scope zero-dispatch PASS, explicit Coach authority and missing/inferred-input rejection, cycle-0 failure/resume, cycle-1 in-boundary remediation, immediate structured/boundary re-slice, valid FAIL durability, process termination, target-asserted reset/clean recovery, commit-without-push recovery, remote restart, and exact model-dispatch counts.

Drive CLI, autonomous-loop, and MCP track merge over disjoint, declared shared, stale-overlap, recoverable local-only synchronization invalidation, no-mutation invalidation blockers, ordinary rollback with a rollback-authored surviving path, post-sync rollback, undeclared-conflict, and idempotent histories and compare refs, parents, trees, statuses, reports, and exit/tool outcomes.

Drive sworn replan apply/MCP apply_plan_delta through an ordinary ratified delta and drive sworn replan migrate/replan activate plus MCP migrate_plan_delta/activate_migrated_plan through partial-propagation restart and all-or-nothing pristine migration of an isolated committed copy of the seven local-first-account-safety records, proving the live downstream release remains unchanged.

Drive merge-release, read-only ship, and post-deployment mark-shipped through normal, tampered, non-deployed, origin-behind/divergent, local-only completed, and already-shipped rerun histories, proving upstream fetch/gate ordering, exact status metadata plus Baton ship/status/index evidence and byte-identical board, local bookkeeping, human push, and regated no-op remain distinct facts.

Record deterministic per-scenario evidence in test failures: invoked public command, redacted stdout/stderr, exit code, dispatch counts, before/after refs, commit parents, tree/blob identities, and release-record hashes.

Prove exact v0.16.0 vendor/archive/isolated-install parity and public board-oracle-v1 aggregate/named compatibility, including malformed topology and unsafe sourceRef failures before a consumer selects a path or Git object.
out_of_scope: This slice changes no production code, protocol contract, schema, installer, command behavior, or release record outside its own planning and proof artefacts.
Tests make no network request to a model provider, Git host, Baton host, notification destination, or deployment service.
A failing conformance scenario is fixed in its owning S01–S17 slice; S18 does not weaken assertions or add compatibility bypasses.
Performance benchmarking, UI board delivery, webhook product delivery, and hosted commercial behavior are outside this conformance proof.

effort_complexity.rationale: The implementation is test-only but spans every public lifecycle, multi-process recovery, multiple Git histories, and exact evidence comparison without external services.

risks.description: A broad end-to-end suite becomes flaky because it depends on global HOME, Git configuration, real remotes, wall clock, or model/network availability.
risks.mitigation: Isolate every process environment and Git config, use local bare remotes and deterministic fake drivers/clocks, prohibit network, and assert no process-global state leaks between scenario tables.

risks.description: The conformance test duplicates internal expectations and passes even though a public adapter bypasses them.
risks.mitigation: Invoke only built-binary and MCP stdio entry points, derive expected identities independently from Git plumbing and committed bytes, and compare all adapters on the same fixtures.

risks.description: A failing scenario is made green by relaxing the final test rather than repairing its owning behavior.
risks.mitigation: Every scenario names its owning C-01 through C-13 or C-18/C-19 contract and S01 through S24 slice; any product mismatch routes back to that owner and the final assertion remains unchanged.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the real-binary complete-PASS scenario installs isolated Codex and Claude mirrors, completes manual S01 and S03-S13 plus S19/S20 evidence, validates terminal S02 as rollback-backed history, integrates T1 then T2 with exact pinned Baton v0.15.1 Track Integrator commits, activates C-13 before creating T5/T6, runs one current-authority role lifecycle, merges its track/release, passes read-only ship, and records deployment with mark-shipped THE SYSTEM SHALL exit 0 only at authorized PASS points and produce exact C-01 through C-13 records, refs, parents, trees, reports, Baton ship blocks, status-derived state/count/activity index views, byte-identical pure-plan board, and terminal states (N-01, N-02, N-03, N-04, N-05, N-06, N-07, N-08, N-09, N-10, N-11).

IF the tamper matrix deletes or rewrites a report, changes overlay provenance/disposition, blob OID, protocol marker, semantic path, fingerprint, lifecycle/Coach member, sync parent, shared result, rollback baseline, readiness fact, deploy containment, or ratified delta source identity THEN the next affected public command SHALL exit non-zero, name the owning C-01 through C-13 gate, perform zero unauthorized model calls, and preserve pre-command refs and bytes (N-01 through N-11).

WHEN the test kills and restarts separate sworn processes at each row of planning/normative-clarifications.md section 10 THE SYSTEM SHALL make standalone stateful commands fail unchanged on uncommitted partial evidence, make resumed loop target-assert then reset/clean every uncommitted leftover before reconstructing committed authority, push only exact C-10/C-13 transitions that authorize push, revalidate C-08 local invalidation without push, return the C-11 already-shipped nothing-to-do no-op without push, create/push only missing deterministic C-12 transaction/activation commits, and block every divergence while matching exact dispatch counts (C-05, C-06, C-08, C-10, C-11, C-12, C-13, N-04, N-05, N-10).

WHEN real-binary and MCP scenarios process disjoint sibling evidence, a C-07 shared file, stale overlap, recognized sync invalidation, the concrete S02→S19 tree-equal rollback followed by fresh S20 authority, an ordinary rollback whose rollback slice adds an otherwise surviving path, post-sync rollback, and an undeclared conflict THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve disjoint bytes, install only the canonical shared blob, commit only the eligible invalidation locally with unchanged remote/release-wt, block later-overlap/unrecognized/shipped invalidation without mutation, require exact full-through-rollback-head or parent-2 equality, and block every ambiguous integration (C-07, C-08, C-09, N-06, N-07, N-08, N-09).

WHEN sworn replan apply and MCP apply_plan_delta receive the same ordinary schema-valid C-12 bytes THE SYSTEM SHALL produce byte-identical unsigned canonical commit objects, receipts, topology, and restart behavior; WHEN migrate/activate adapters use the complete 17-mutation local-first manifest against an isolated SHA-1 copy THE SYSTEM SHALL update all seven records plus contracts consumers or none, reproduce every section 11 golden blob/tree/commit OID including exact timezone/message bytes, separately activate authority, add no synthetic evidence, retire direct MCP plan_release/create_slice/set_track/update_intake writes, pass every gate, and leave the live downstream release byte-identical (C-02, C-03, C-12, N-02, N-10, N-11).

WHEN the same valid release is checked before deployment, after a deployed commit containing its release merge, after mark-shipped, and on an already-shipped rerun THE SYSTEM SHALL keep ship read-only, make mark-shipped fetch origin and revalidate exact upstream gates, create one local exact last_updated plus Baton ship/status/index commit with byte-identical pure-plan board, leave remotes unchanged, return the exact handoff once, then return the fully regated no-deployment-evidence nothing-to-do no-op; IF fetch/remote ancestry, current C-09 authority, terminal record validity, or board immutability is absent THEN neither shipped nor no-op success SHALL occur; IF a verified slice remains and the deployed commit is absent/invalid or fails to contain an existing conventional release-merge identity THEN the transition SHALL block, while absence of that identity SHALL record Baton's legacy containment-skip note; no command SHALL push or clean automatically (C-09, C-11, N-09, N-10).

WHEN public maintainability adjudication receives explicit Coach decision, rationale, identity, and resume-only permissions THE SYSTEM SHALL derive report citations/blocking IDs, capture time once, and preserve that immutable authority through restart; IF any Coach field is missing/inferred or cycle-1 structured disposition/Git-authored paths exceed permitted_touchpoints THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL block before unauthorized mutation and perform zero boundary-expanding remediation, closure, Verifier, or second-Coach dispatches across CLI loop, router, scheduler, and MCP-visible state (C-05, C-06, C-10, N-04, N-05, N-09, N-10).

WHEN public maintainability review receives an eligible semantic scope with zero included paths THE SYSTEM SHALL persist the exact deterministic PASS report and lifecycle transition, exit 0, invoke neither Git diff nor a model driver, and preserve the same report identity and zero dispatch count across restart (C-04, C-05, C-10, N-03, N-10).

WHEN the real binary runs the isolated v0.16.0 parity and board scenarios THE SYSTEM SHALL prove tagged archive and Codex/Claude installation parity, emit valid aggregate and named board-oracle-v1 compatibility JSON, and reject duplicate ownership, inconsistent dependencies, malformed sourceRef, or mutable empty-source fallback before a path or Git object is selected (C-18, C-19, N-15, N-16).
